title: Aphyllon corymbosum
intro: Aphyllon corymbosum, also known as Liatris scabra, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aphyllon corymbosum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Aphyllon corymbosum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is used to treat conditions such as sore throats, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Aphyllon corymbosum is a plant species, and it belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is further classified as a genus within the Apocynaceae family. Aphyllon corymbosum is native to the Americas and is also known as the "yellow jessamine" or "yellow jimsonweed."
